title: Comforting Middle Eastern Apricot Tagine
description: A sweet and savory dish featuring dried apricots, perfect for a sick day, made with halal-friendly ingredients and a poaching method to create a jammy and gooey texture.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the chicken breast and cook until it is browned on all sides and cooked through.
Add the dried apricots, cumin, cinnamon, chicken broth, and honey, stirring to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 30 minutes.
Remove the lid and continue to simmer for an additional 10 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened and the apricots are jammy and gooey.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs, if desired.
